description:
First subticket of fz-ul4.10. Implement the encoding from AST nodes (`Expr`, `Pattern`) to fz Values and back, so quote can produce a real fz value and the expansion pass can decode a macro's return value into an AST node.

Encoding choice: Elixir-style 3-tuple {name, meta, args} for calls/ops, with vars as {name, meta, ctx}. Literals stay literal (an int 42 is just Value::Int(42), not wrapped). meta is a map (line/col); for v1 keep it empty %{}.

Examples:
- 42                  -> Value::Int(42)
- 1 + 2               -> Value::Tuple([:+, %{}, [1, 2]])
- foo(1)              -> Value::Tuple([:foo, %{}, [1]])
- x                   -> Value::Tuple([:x, %{}, :Elixir])    # var
- [1, 2]              -> Value::List([1, 2])
- {a, b}              -> Value::Tuple([{:"{}", %{}, [a, b]}]) — bikeshed at impl

Deliverable:
- Module ast_value (or in ast.rs): `fn expr_to_value(e: &Expr) -> Value` and `fn value_to_expr(v: &Value) -> Result<Expr, String>`.
- Round-trip tests for each Expr variant we care about (lits, vars, BinOp, Call, List, Tuple, If, Block).
- Patterns can wait if scoped narrowly — file follow-up if needed.

No lexer / parser changes here. quote/unquote syntax lives in .10.2.
title: AST reification: Expr <-> Value encoding
